## About

Atelier is a free, open-source desktop app that brings multi-provider AI conversations directly into your project files. Open any folder, ask questions, get cited answers, and let the AI create or edit files — all without sending your data to our servers.

We believe AI should be accessible to everyone, not just people who can write code or configure a vector database. Atelier is designed for non-technical people as much as for developers, so anyone can take part in the shift instead of being left behind by it.

## What we value

- **Privacy by design** — Your files stay on your machine. No accounts, no telemetry, no data sent to us.
- **Open source first** — The desktop app is open-source so anyone can audit, fork, and verify what it does.
- **Bring your own AI** — Use OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, local models via Ollama, or any provider you prefer.
- **Cited & verifiable** — Every response includes source citations so you know what to trust.
- **No vendor lock-in** — Your data lives in a local SQLite database. Your credentials are encrypted on disk.

## Repositories

| Repository | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| [`open-atelier-app`](https://github.com/Open-Atelier-App/open-atelier-app) | The Atelier desktop app — a local-first AI workspace built with Tauri, React, and Rust. |

More projects and tooling will land here as the ecosystem grows.

## Roadmap

- ✅ Open-source desktop app for Mac and Linux
- 🚧 Windows support
- 🚧 Web beta for no-install, collaborative workspaces

The desktop app will always remain free and open-source. The upcoming web beta will be a paid, hosted service that helps fund ongoing development.

## Get involved

- **Try the app** — Download the latest release from [open-atelier.app](https://open-atelier.app).
- **Read the docs** — Learn how to install, configure providers, and use skills at [doc.open-atelier.app](https://doc.open-atelier.app).
- **Report feedback** — Open an issue or share feedback through [open-atelier.app/insights](https://open-atelier.app/insights).
- **Support development** — Help fund the web beta via [Buy Me a Coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/openatelier).
